name: agent-cf-planner

criteria.name: approach_diversity
criteria.description: Generates multiple distinct approaches rather than just one

scoring.0: Only one approach presented with no alternatives considered
scoring.1: Two approaches but they are minor variations of the same idea
scoring.2: Two genuinely different approaches with some trade-off analysis
scoring.3: Three or more meaningfully different approaches with clear differentiation

criteria.name: pros_cons_depth
criteria.description: Each approach has substantive pros and cons analysis

scoring.0: No pros/cons analysis for any approach
scoring.1: Pros/cons listed but they are generic (e.g., 'faster' / 'harder') without specifics
scoring.2: Good pros/cons for some approaches but superficial for others
scoring.3: Every approach has specific, project-relevant pros and cons with concrete trade-offs

criteria.name: task_actionability
criteria.description: Tasks in the recommended approach are specific enough to implement directly

scoring.0: Tasks are vague (e.g., 'implement the feature') with no file or function targets
scoring.1: Some tasks are actionable but others require further decomposition
scoring.2: Most tasks are actionable with target files but some lack specificity
scoring.3: Every task specifies target files, expected changes, and verification steps

criteria.name: risk_coverage
criteria.description: Identifies risks, dependencies, and potential blockers for the recommended approach

scoring.1: One obvious risk mentioned but no mitigation or dependency analysis
scoring.2: Key risks identified with mitigations but missing dependency analysis
scoring.3: Comprehensive risk analysis with mitigations, dependencies mapped, and fallback plan

notes: Planner should explore the codebase before planning to ground approaches in reality. A recommendation for which approach to use should be included with clear reasoning. Small tasks may only warrant 2 approaches.
